claim: Modern TTS models use autoregressive transformers that generate audio tokens sequentially, following the LLM architecture pattern
actionable: Use autoregressive token-based architectures when designing TTS systems to leverage well-established sequence modeling techniques
source_title: Why TTS Models Now Look Like LLMs — Samuel Humeau, Mistral

claim: Neural audio codecs solve the information density problem by compressing ~200kbps of audio signal into a manageable token rate (~500 tokens/second)
actionable: Implement neural audio codecs with lossy compression to reduce audio from 200kbps to 500 tokens/sec, enabling efficient transformer processing
source_title: Why TTS Models Now Look Like LLMs — Samuel Humeau, Mistral

claim: Voice cloning can work with only a few seconds of reference audio, enabling rapid voice identity capture
actionable: Use short audio samples (seconds, not minutes) as conditioning inputs when implementing voice cloning features in TTS systems
source_title: Why TTS Models Now Look Like LLMs — Samuel Humeau, Mistral

claim: Streaming audio generation (playing first packets before full generation) significantly reduces perceived latency in agent conversations
actionable: Implement streaming audio output so users hear initial audio packets within 17ms, reducing perceived latency even if full generation takes minutes
source_title: Why TTS Models Now Look Like LLMs — Samuel Humeau, Mistral

claim: Real-time text-to-speech agents require solving streaming text input from LLMs (tokens arriving in real-time), which is still an open problem without a clear winner approach
actionable: Be aware that handling real-time streaming text input to TTS remains unsolved; current models either generate per-utterance or use ad-hoc stitching with continuity issues
source_title: Why TTS Models Now Look Like LLMs — Samuel Humeau, Mistral

claim: Codec tokens are generated in parallel within each 80ms frame (37 tokens at once) using diffusion models, diverging from vanilla autoregressive token generation
actionable: Consider diffusion-based parallel token generation per frame instead of sequential token generation to improve inference speed
source_title: Why TTS Models Now Look Like LJMs — Samuel Humeau, Mistral
